title: cryptotaenia androphila
intro: Cryptotaenia androphila, also known as Japanese parsley or gosho-bai-tsuryo, is a species of plant in the carrot family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu, as well as acting as a digestive aid and antioxidant. In herbal preparations, Cryptotaenia androphila is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, and is also used in traditional Asian medicine to treat various ailments.
